announcement.goals.gexpress.bomb_special: Plant explosives on your targets and eliminate everyone aboard.
announcement.goals.gexpress.medic: Give a passenger a protective shield and keep the train alive.
announcement.goals.gexpress.snitch: Complete tasks to uncover the killers hiding aboard.
announcement.goals.gexpress.seer: Feel every death on the train and warn the passengers before it is too late.
announcement.goals.gexpress.time_master: Turn back the last few seconds when the train falls apart.
announcement.goals.gexpress.the_silent: Disappear into Shadow March, strike without footsteps, then snap back before the train can pin you down.
announcement.goals.gexpress.warlock: Mark a player and use their position to kill someone standing too close to them.
announcement.goals.gexpress.juggernaut: You are alone. Outlast every passenger and every killer.
announcement.goals.gexpress.trickster: Scramble identities, silence the train, dance the middle carts around, and kill while everyone doubts what they saw.
announcement.goals.gexpress.puppetmaster: Take control of living players and turn the train against itself.
announcement.goals.gexpress.pelican: Swallow living players, keep them hidden, and eat 80% of the lobby to win alone.
announcement.goals.gexpress.scatter_brai: Scramble the entire train, then use the panic to kill.
announcement.goals.gexpress.tracker: Mark suspects and keep eyes on the people who matter.
announcement.goals.gexpress.altruist: Trade your life to bring a dead passenger back.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.role.desc.ge: A demolition expert who treats every passenger as a potential blast radius. Armed with custom-made C4, the Bomb Specialist plants explosives on their victims, setting each one to go out with a deadly click.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.role.abiliti:
{icon:instinct} §lInstinct§r
See players and §4Killer Cohorts§r through walls.

{icon:athletic} §lAthletic§r
Has infinite stamina.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.role.desc.ge: A civilian support role that can place a protective shield on one passenger. The Medic sees their protected target through walls and gets a warning flash when the shield is hit.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.role.abiliti:
{icon:sense} §lShield§r
Press the ability key while looking at a nearby player to shield them. Knives are blocked, and the first gunshot breaks the shield instead of killing. Hosts can configure whether knives also break the shield.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.role.desc.ge: A civilian investigator who can expose the killers by getting work done under pressure. Once enough tasks are completed, the Snitch privately learns who the living killers are.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.role.abiliti:
{icon:instinct} §lEvidence§r
Complete the configured number of tasks to reveal the living killers and their roles.

{icon:sense} §lKiller Read§r
After the reveal, aim at a killer to see their exact role.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.role.desc.ge: A civilian sensitive to deaths aboard the train. Whenever someone dies, the Seer gets a sudden red flash.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.role.abiliti:
{icon:sense} §lDeath Sense§r
When another player dies, your screen flashes red.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.role.desc.ge: A civilian with a dangerous emergency reset. The Time Master can pull the active round back by the configured number of seconds, reviving anyone who was alive at that moment and undoing recent purchases, inventory changes, coins, positions, status effects, cooldowns, and G'Express ability state.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.role.abiliti:
{icon:sense} §lRewind§r
Press the ability key to return the round to a recent snapshot. Each use has a configured cooldown and each Time Master only has the configured number of total uses.

{icon:undercover} §lFreeze§r
Press Secondary Ability while looking at a nearby player to lock them in place for the configured duration. Freeze has its own configured cooldown.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.role.desc.ge: A killer who turns a single hallway into a bad memory. The Silent can vanish into a black translucent shadow, move faster, and leave no footsteps behind.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.role.abiliti:
{icon:instinct} §lInstinct§r
See players and §4Killer Cohorts§r through walls.

{icon:athletic} §lAthletic§r
Has infinite stamina.

{icon:instinct} §lShadow March§r
Press the ability key to enter shadow form. When the timer ends, you return to the spot where you started.

{icon:athletic} §lQuiet Step§r
While Shadow March is active, your footsteps are muted and you move a little faster.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.role.desc.ge: A killer who curses one player at a time and attacks through them. The Warlock does not need to stand beside the victim; they need their marked player to be close enough instead.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.role.abiliti:
{icon:instinct} §lInstinct§r
See players and §4Killer Cohorts§r through walls.

{icon:athletic} §lAthletic§r
Has infinite stamina.

{icon:instinct} §lMark§r
Press the ability key while looking at a nearby player to mark them. Only one player can be marked at a time. Marking a different player starts a retarget delay.

{icon:knife} §lHex Kill§r
After you have a mark, a knife omen appears above your hotbar. It turns red when another player is close enough to your marked player. Press Secondary Ability to kill one random nearby player through the mark.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.role.desc.ge: A neutral solo role that starts armed but slow to strike. The Juggernaut wins only by being the last player left alive.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.role.abiliti:
{icon:athletic} §lAthletic§r
Has infinite stamina.

{icon:knife} §lArmed Alone§r
Starts with a Knife and a Revolver. Both share a very long opening cooldown.

{icon:icon_weapon_cooldown_refresh} §lMomentum§r
Each Juggernaut kill shortens future weapon cooldowns until the configured minimum is reached.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.role.desc.ge: A killer who turns the whole train into unreliable testimony. The Harlequin can temporarily make players appear as other players while the voice chat goes silent, then rearrange the train's middle carts to scatter the scene.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.role.abiliti:
{icon:instinct} §lInstinct§r
See players and §4Killer Cohorts§r through walls.

{icon:athletic} §lAthletic§r
Has infinite stamina.

{icon:swap} §lMasquerade§r
Press the ability key to shuffle every living player's skin into someone else's skin for the configured duration.

{icon:undercover} §lDead Air§r
While Masquerade is active, voice chat is muted globally.

{icon:swap} §lDancing Carts§r
Press Secondary Ability to shuffle the configured middle train carts. The front and back carts stay fixed, and entities inside the moved carts travel with them.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.role.desc.ge: A killer who turns passengers into unwilling instruments. The Puppetmaster can look through another living player's eyes and steer them for a short, dangerous moment.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.role.abiliti:
{icon:instinct} §lInstinct§r
See players and §4Killer Cohorts§r through walls.

{icon:athletic} §lAthletic§r
Has infinite stamina.

{icon:undercover} §lPuppet Strings§r
Press the ability key to open a list of living players. Choose one to control their movement through their eyes.

{icon:instinct} §lPossession§r
The controlled player receives a red overlay until the Puppetmaster releases them.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.role.desc.ge: A neutral solo role that swallows living players whole. Eaten players are stashed inside the Pelican, can only talk within the belly channel, and are spat out if the Pelican releases them or dies.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.role.abiliti:
{icon:athletic} §lAthletic§r
Has infinite stamina.

{icon:instinct} §lSwallow§r
Press the ability key while looking at a nearby living player to stash them inside your body.

{icon:swap} §lSpit Out§r
Press Secondary Ability to release one swallowed player at your feet. Eat 80% of the lobby to win alone.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.role.desc.ge: A killer who turns positioning into chaos. Scatter Brain can throw every living player to valid random spots around the active map while keeping their current height.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.role.abiliti:
{icon:instinct} §lInstinct§r
See players and §4Killer Cohorts§r through walls.

{icon:athletic} §lAthletic§r
Has infinite stamina.

{icon:swap} §lScatter§r
Press the ability key to teleport all living players, including yourself, to valid safe positions on the map.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.role.desc.ge: A civilian investigator who can keep a small list of suspects highlighted. Tracked players glow only for the Tracker.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.role.abiliti:
{icon:instinct} §lTrack§r
Press the ability key while looking at a nearby living player to toggle tracking on them. The Tracker can keep up to the configured number of players tracked at once.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.role.desc.ge: A civilian support role that can bring someone back at the highest possible cost. The Altruist sacrifices themself to revive a dead body.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.role.abiliti:
{icon:sense} §lRevive§r
Press the ability key while looking at a nearby body to revive that player and die in their place.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.modifier.des: A trained bomb-disposal technician. Starts the game with a pair of Pliers that can defuse C4 stuck to any player - but a shaky hand can clip the wrong wire and set off the charge on the spot.
Your eyes aren't what they used to be. Players, dropped items, and bodies beyond a few paces slip out of sight.
Your eyes adjust to the dark. While the modifier is active, you can see clearly in low light.
You can carry extra food from platters. Hosts configure how many food items Hungry players may hold at once.
You can carry extra drinks from trays. Hosts configure how many drink items Thirsty players may hold at once.
You can hear voice chat, but your own microphone is blocked.
You always imagine players holding weapons, even when your sanity is not low.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.item.c4.desc: Right-click on a player to stick it on their back. Beeps louder and faster as its timer counts down, then explodes in a blast that kills anyone nearby - including the carrier.
gui.watheextended.guidebook.item.c4_deto: Right-click to arm every C4 charge you have thrown. Once armed, the charges wait for the configured first-beep delay and fuse before exploding.
Right-click on a player carrying a C4 to attempt a defuse. A successful clip removes the charge; a bad one detonates it. The Pliers are single-use.
gui.watheextended.config.option.gexpress: C4 Price

How many coins the Bomb Specialist pays for one C4 charge in the shop.

§6Default: §e500
C4 Fuse Time

How many seconds the C4 beeps for after the first-beep delay before detonating.

§6Default: §e15
First Beep Delay

How many seconds after planting a C4 charge pass before it starts beeping.

§6Default: §e3
Wrong Wire Chance

Percent chance a Pliers defuse attempt clips the wrong wire and instantly detonates the C4.

§6Default: §e20
Grenade Price

How many coins the Bomb Specialist pays for one Grenade in the shop.

§6Default: §e150
Shield Cooldown

How many seconds a Medic must wait before shielding another player.

§6Default: §e90
Knife Breaks Shield

If enabled, a blocked knife hit consumes the Medic shield. If disabled, knives are blocked without breaking it.

§6Default: §eDisabled
Shadow March Duration

How many seconds The Silent stays in shadow form before being returned to the starting spot.

§6Default: §e8
Shadow March Cooldown

How many seconds The Silent must wait after Shadow March ends before using it again.

§6Default: §e35
Mark Cooldown

How many seconds a Warlock must wait before moving their mark to another player.

§6Default: §e20
Hex Kill Cooldown

How many seconds a Warlock must wait after forcing a marked-player kill.

§6Default: §e45
Starting Weapon Cooldown

How many seconds the Juggernaut's Knife and Revolver are locked at the start of the round.

§6Default: §e60
Cooldown Reduction Per Kill

How many seconds are removed from the Juggernaut's weapon cooldown after each kill.

§6Default: §e10
Minimum Weapon Cooldown

The lowest cooldown the Juggernaut's weapons can reach after repeated kills.

§6Default: §e10
Masquerade Duration

How many seconds the Harlequin's skin shuffle and global voice mute last.

§6Default: §e25
Dancing Carts Uses

How many times each Harlequin can shuffle the train carts per round.

§6Default: §e1
Control Duration

How many seconds the Puppetmaster can steer a selected player.

§6Default: §e15
Control Cooldown

How many seconds the Puppetmaster must wait after a control ends.

§6Default: §e45
Random Puppet Target

If enabled, the ability immediately controls a random living player instead of opening the target menu.

§6Default: §eDisabled
Control Range

How close a living player must be to appear in the Puppetmaster target menu.

§6Default: §e16
Can Kill Own Body

If enabled, the controlled puppet can kill the Puppetmaster's abandoned body. Control ends immediately if that happens.

§6Default: §eDisabled
Swallow Cooldown

How many seconds the Pelican must wait after swallowing a player before swallowing again.

§6Default: §e20
Food Carry Limit

How many food items a Hungry player may carry from platters at once.

§6Default: §e2
Drink Carry Limit

How many drink items a Thirsty player may carry from trays at once.

§6Default: §e2
Tasks To Reveal

How many completed tasks a Snitch needs before seeing the living killers.

§6Default: §e3
Killer Warning Tasks Left

How many tasks away from finishing the Snitch becomes visible to killers. Set to 0 to disable the killer warning and glow.

§6Default: §e1
Rewind Time

How many seconds the Time Master rewinds the active round.

§6Default: §e10
Rewind Cooldown

How many seconds a Time Master must wait after rewinding before using it again.

§6Default: §e120
Max Rewinds

How many times each Time Master can rewind in one round. Set to 0 to make the role unable to use the ability.

§6Default: §e1
Freeze Duration

How many seconds a Time Master freezes their target.

§6Default: §e4
Freeze Cooldown

How many seconds a Time Master must wait after freezing a player before freezing again.

§6Default: §e30
Freeze Range

How far a Time Master can reach when freezing the player they are looking at.

§6Default: §e8
Scatter Cooldown

How many seconds Scatter Brain must wait after scattering the train.

§6Default: §e60
Tracked Players

How many players the Tracker can keep glowing at once.

§6Default: §e3
Track Range

How far the Tracker can reach when selecting a player.

§6Default: §e24
Track Cooldown

How many seconds the Tracker must wait after toggling a tracked player.

§6Default: §e10
Revive Range

How close the Altruist must be to a body to revive it.

§6Default: §e4
Last Death Shield

If enabled, players who died in the previous round start the next round with a one-hit shield.

§6Default: §eDisabled
Entity Visibility Range

Entities, dropped items, and bodies beyond this distance are hidden while Short-sighted is active.

§6Default: §e5.000
gui.watheextended.config.title: G'Express Options
gui.gexpress.puppetmaster.title: Choose a Puppet
gui.gexpress.puppetmaster.subtitle: Select a living player to control
gui.gexpress.config.title: G'Express Options
gui.gexpress.config.group.maps.list.tool: Click a preset name to edit its values.
gui.gexpress.config.maps.back.tooltip: Return to the map presets list.
gui.gexpress.config.category.game.toolti: Host-only game settings, roles, and modifiers.
gui.gexpress.config.category.dev.tooltip: Greyifer-only tuning values for testing visuals without editing code.
gui.gexpress.config.category.train_carts: Edit Dancing Carts regions for the train preset assigned to each map.
Tunable values for G'Express content (prices, timers, chances).
gui.gexpress.config.group.dev.c4_back_mo: Client-side transform values for the C4 model rendered on a player's back.
gui.gexpress.config.group.dev.role_descr: Overrides for the role descriptions shown in G'Express role option tooltips.
gui.gexpress.config.group.dev.short_sigh: Client-side entity visibility distance for the Short-sighted modifier.
gui.gexpress.config.group.dev.medic_shie: Client-side flash overlay values for Medic shield hits.
gui.gexpress.config.group.dev.silent_sha: Client-side opacity tuning for The Silent's shadow form.
gui.gexpress.config.group.client.ability: Client-side layout values for the ability cooldown bars shown around the hotbar.
gui.gexpress.config.option.client.abilit:
Scales the ability cooldown bar and its icon.

§6Default: §e100%

Moves the ability cooldown bars left or right from their default position beside the hotbar.

§6Default: §e0 px

Moves the ability cooldown bars up or down from their default position above the hotbar edge.

§6Default: §e0 px
gui.gexpress.config.option.dev.c4_back_o:
Sideways model offset in player body-local space.

§6Default: §e0.000

Vertical model offset in player body-local space. Lower values move the C4 upward.

§6Default: §e0.240

Depth model offset in player body-local space. Higher values push the C4 farther off the back.

§6Default: §e0.280
gui.gexpress.config.option.dev.c4_back_r:
Pitch rotation in degrees after the model is moved onto the back.

§6Default: §e0.000

Yaw rotation in degrees after the model is moved onto the back.

§6Default: §e0.000

Roll rotation in degrees after the model is moved onto the back.

§6Default: §e0.000
gui.gexpress.config.option.dev.c4_back_s:
Extra diagonal tilt in degrees for making the C4 sit slightly slanted across the back.

§6Default: §e0.000

Uniform size of the back-mounted C4 model.

§6Default: §e0.420
gui.gexpress.config.option.dev.c4_placem: Optional placement presets for player and surface C4. Each row is: x y z rotX rotY rotZ slant scale. Adding a row snapshots the current C4 sliders. When multiple rows exist, a random row is chosen when C4 sticks.
gui.gexpress.config.option.dev.role_desc: Custom description text for this G'Express role. Leave empty to use the built-in text. Use \n for line breaks.
gui.gexpress.config.option.dev.short_sig:
Entities, dropped items, and bodies beyond this distance are hidden while Short-sighted is active.

§6Default: §e5.000
gui.gexpress.config.option.dev.medic_shi:
How long the Medic's screen flash lasts when a shield blocks without breaking.

§6Default: §e18

How long the Medic's screen flash lasts when a shield breaks.

§6Default: §e28

Maximum green overlay opacity when a shield blocks without breaking.

§6Default: §e72

Maximum green overlay opacity when a shield breaks.

§6Default: §e92
gui.gexpress.config.option.dev.silent_sh:
Opacity for The Silent's black shadow form. Lower values are more transparent.

§6Default: §e0.450
gui.gexpress.config.group.modifiers.tool: Toggle game modifiers. Hover an entry to see its description and options.
gui.gexpress.config.option.tuning.role_c: Percent chance this enabled role is allowed to appear when a game starts.
gui.gexpress.config.option.tuning.role_m: Amount of players that may get this role in one game. Set to 0 to stop normal assignment without disabling the role.
gui.gexpress.config.option.max_killer_am: Maximum total killer-team players G'Express may assign in one game. Forced roles can still exceed this cap. Set to 64 for effectively unlimited.
gui.gexpress.config.option.max_vigilante: Maximum Vigilantes G'Express may assign in one game. Set to 0 to prevent normal Vigilante assignment.
gui.gexpress.config.option.tuning.modifi: Percent chance this enabled modifier is allowed to appear when a game starts.
Amount of players that may get this modifier in one game. Set to 0 to stop normal assignment without disabling the modifier.
gui.gexpress.config.category.maps.toolti: Edit saved map presets stored in this world.
gui.gexpress.config.group.train_carts.li: Choose a map to edit the train carts on its default train preset.
gui.gexpress.config.group.train_carts.no: This map has no default train preset.
gui.gexpress.config.group.train_carts.mi: The assigned train preset was not found.
gui.gexpress.config.group.train_carts.ca: Each row is: x1 y1 z1 x2 y2 z2. Cart #1 and the last cart stay fixed; Dancing Carts shuffles the middle carts.
gui.gexpress.config.group.maps.empty.too: Save a preset with /g map create <corner1> <corner2> <name> to see it here.
gui.gexpress.config.group.map.tooltip: Edit values for map preset '%s'. Changes persist when you click Save.
gui.gexpress.config.group.map.randomSpaw: Player-spawn slots used for random teleports. Each entry is 'x y z yaw pitch'. Use the entry delete controls to remove individual spots.
gui.gexpress.config.maps.randomSpawns.im: On save, copies the world's current /g rtp slots into this map preset.
gui.gexpress.config.maps.randomSpawns.cl: On save, removes every random spawn position from this map preset.
gui.gexpress.config.option.map.box.toolt: Enter as 'minX minY minZ maxX maxY maxZ' (six numbers separated by spaces).
gui.gexpress.config.option.map.pos.toolt: Enter as 'x y z yaw pitch' (five numbers separated by spaces).
gui.gexpress.config.option.map.offset.to: Enter as 'x y z' (three integers separated by spaces).
gui.gexpress.config.option.map.weather.t: Weather to apply while this preset is active.
gui.gexpress.config.option.map.defaultTr: Name of the train preset that loads with this map. Empty = none.
